sutta: now brahmin januszoni visited the exalted 1 and said to him does master gautama profess to live the holy life and the buddha said brahman of whomsoever speaking rightly 1 may say he lives the holy life unbroken without rent untarnished without blemish all and pure of me verily speaking rightly may he say for i indeed live the holy life unbroken without rent and tarnished without blemish whole and pure but what master gautama is the breaking rending tarnishing blemish of the holy life and the buddha said consider brahmin some recluse of brahmin professing to live the holy life in full who in sooth falls not so far as to couple with women folk yet enjoys being rubbed massaged bathed shampooed by a woman relishes it longs for it and is entranced thereby this indeed brahman is the breaking rending tarnishing blemish of the holy life this man brahman is said to live the holy life impurely bound by the bond of sex nor is he free from being born aging dying from sorrow lamentation pain grief despair he is not freed from ill i say again consider some recluse of brahmin who in sooth falls not so far as to couple with womenful nor enjoys being rubbed massaged and so forth yet he jokes just makes mary with them and then again though he do none of these things yet eye on eye burns for them stares after them again though he do none of these things yet he listens to them as they laugh talk sing or weep beyond the wall beyond the fence again though he do none of these things yet he remembers the laughs talks chests he had with them before although he do none of these things yet he watches some layman or layman's son bent on and grossed in reveling in the 5 strands of sensual pleasure although he do none of these things yet he lives the holy life in hope of a deva body thinking by this virtue vow penance holy life i shall become a deva or 1 of the deva host religious it longs for it his entrance thereby this indeed brahman is the breaking rending tarnishing blemish of the holy life this man brahman is said to live the holy life impurely bound by the bond of sex nor is he freed from being born ageing dying from sorrow lamentation pain grief despair he is not freed from ill or dukkha i say and so long brahmin as i continue to see 1 or the other of these 7 sex bonds in myself not got rid of i professed not to be wholly awakened to the highest and full awakening unsurpassed in the world with its devas maras brahmas on earth with its recluses brahmins devas and men but when i saw no bond of sex not got rid of then i professed to be wholly awakened to the highest and full awakening unsurpassed in the world unsurpassed on earth then to me knowledge insight arose immovable is my liberation by mine this is my last birth there is no more becoming here again and when he had does spoken brahmin janissoni said to the exalted 1 it's amazing master gotama etc etc and he became a lay disciple of the buddha as the end of the suta
commentary:
so here the buddha is describing the various stages of how this uh bond of the opposite sex can uh a monk and cling tool here he talks about the monk who enjoys being rubbed massage paid shampooed by a woman this cannot be a buddhism this probably could refer to a brahmin because brahmins were originally supposed to be renuncians later because they were taken to be advisers of the kings they were given a lot of landed property and given slaves and even wives and other property so some of them they would enjoy being robbed massage bait and shampooed by a woman and even if a monk does not do this yet he could uh enjoy joking with women and then again even if he does not joke directly with women he likes to stare at women and then even if he does not do all these things yet he likes to listen to them as they laugh talk or sing are we beyond the monastery wall or beyond the monastery friends or even though if you do none of all these things yet he remembers the last talks and talks he had with women before or he does none of these things and then he likes to watch some laymen enjoying the sensual pleasures or lastly he may hope to be reborn as a deva these are all gradations of the bond of attraction of the opposite sex in the holy life in the monks precepts the first 1 in other words 1 of the most important ones in the mountains precepts is the preceptor against sexual conduct in christianity this would be called the vow of chastity and a spiritual life is the giving up of sensual desires and of all the sensual desires and the sexual desire is the strongest and his cause for rebirth uh towards the end of the sutta the buddha mentioned that immovable is my liberation by mine [Music] otherwise the buddha is saying here that he was liberated by mind and in the majima nikaya number 71 the buddha says that he was liberated by mind and liberated by wisdom which means to say that person who is liberated by mine is also a person who is liberated by mind and wisdom because in the description of the 7 types of arya the 7 types of arya the first 1 is mentioned as it is 2 ways liberated arahant and then the second 1 is liberated by wisdom so there is no mention of liberation by mind so from here we can know that liberation by mine is equivalent to liberation by mind and liberation by wisdom which is a 2-way liberated arahant
